children.label: A
children.text: compliance with section 11401 of the Fixing America’s Surface Transportation Act and ; andsection 130(g) of title 23

children.label: B
children.text: the specific strategies identified by each State to improve safety at highway-rail grade crossings, including crossings with multiple accidents or incidents;
the progress of each State in implementing its State highway-rail grade crossings action plan;

the number of highway-rail grade crossing projects undertaken pursuant to , including the distribution of such projects by cost range, road system, nature of treatment, and subsequent accident experience at improved locations;section 130 of title 23
which States are not in compliance with their schedule of projects under ; andsection 130(d) of title 23
any recommendations for future implementation of the railway-highway crossings program under .section 130 of title 23

update the report based on the State annual reports submitted pursuant to and any other information obtained by or available to the Administrator of the Federal Railroad Administration; andsection 130(g) of title 23
submit the updated report to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: a public highway, road, or street, or a private roadway, including associated sidewalks and pathways, crosses 1 or more railroad tracks, either at grade or grade-separated; or

children.label: i
children.text: is dedicated for the use of nonvehicular traffic, including pedestrians, bicyclists, and others;

children.label: ii
children.text: is not associated with a public highway, road, or street, or a private roadway; and

children.label: iii
children.text: crosses 1 or more railroad tracks, either at grade or grade-separated.

children.heading: .—State
children.label: 2 .—State
children.text: The term “State” means a State of the United States or the District of Columbia.
